name: Hy3
archDisplay: Mixture-of-Experts hybrid fast/slow-thinking model, 295B total / 21B active
note: Full release of Tencent's Hunyuan 3 (upgraded from an April 2026 preview build), Tencent's first genuinely unrestricted Apache 2.0 flagship. Newer than Hunyuan-A13B already in corpus (dated 2025-06-27). Pricing converted from RMB 1/RMB 4 per million input/output tokens at ~7.1 RMB/USD.

results.notes: GPQA Diamond, from Tencent's own Hy3-preview model card (295B-A21B); full July release benchmark table not separately located [Corrected during full-corpus audit: The existing 87.2 figure was pulled from the April-2026 Hy3-preview build's GitHub model card (explicitly flagged in the prior verificationNotes as a stand-in because 'the full July release benchmark table' hadn't been located). That table has now been located: the official tencent/Hy3 model card's benchmark-appendix image reports GPQA Diamond = 90.4 for the actual shipped Hy3 (295B-A21B) model, v]
SWE-bench Verified; also confirmed via HF's structured Evaluation-results widget (SWE-bench/SWE-bench_Verified = 78) and the model card's benchmark-appendix image
Confirmed via both HF's Evaluation-results widget (ScaleAI/SWE-bench_Pro = 57.9) and the benchmark-appendix image
Terminal-Bench 2.1, from the model card's benchmark-appendix image (Agentic Coding section)
BrowseComp, from the benchmark-appendix image (Agentic Search section)

HLE, no-tools/text-only variant, listed under the appendix's 'Reasoning' task category (a separate 'HLE with tools' row under 'STEM Agent' scores 53.2, but that is a distinct tool-augmented setup, not standard HLE)

USAMO 2026, from Tencent's official Hy3 model-card benchmark table (STEM/Reasoning section). Not previously tracked for this model. Independently corroborated verbatim (72.0) by a second outlet, valueaddvc.com ('Tencent's Hy3 Beats GLM-5.2 at Half the Size'), both citing the same official figure.

verificationNotes:
Benchmark numbers (MMLU 87.42, MMLU-Pro 65.76, GPQA Diamond 87.2, LiveCodeBench-v6 34.86) come from the preview build's GitHub model card (Tencent-Hunyuan/Hy3-preview), which Tencent itself published -- treated as self-reported since it is the vendor's own repo, and may differ slightly from the July full-release numbers which were not separately published with a benchmark table in sources checked.

2026-07-16: Resolved the six missing-core-benchmark gaps (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval, livecodebench, mmmu) for the full July-2026 Hy3 release by downloading and directly inspecting the official model card's two benchmark images (huggingface.co/tencent/Hy3, assets/benchmark.png and assets/benchmark-appendix.png). The appendix table is comprehensive (6 task categories: Agentic Coding, Agentic Search, Working Agent, STEM Agent, Reasoning, Context Learning; ~30 named benchmarks with Hy3-preview vs. Hy3 columns) and deliberately does not include MMLU, MATH, AIME, HumanEval, LiveCodeBench, or MMMU anywhere -- Tencent replaced the legacy academic set with newer evals (USAMO 2026, MathArena Apex, ArxivMath, HorizonMath, IMOAnswerBench, PHYBench, CMT-Benchmark, SuperChem for math/reasoning; SWE-bench Multilingual/Verified/Pro, Terminal-Bench 2.1, NL2repo, DeepSWE for coding) for the shipped model's official comparison. Cross-referenced the April-2026 predecessor checkpoint's own GitHub README (Tencent-Hunyuan/Hy3-preview, raw README fetched directly): it does report MMLU 87.42 (5-shot), MMLU-Redux 86.86 (5-shot), MMLU-Pro 65.76 (5-shot), MATH 76.28 (4-shot), GSM8K 95.37 (4-shot), and LiveCodeBench-v6 34.86 (1-shot) -- but these describe a distinct predecessor checkpoint, not the shipped Hy3, and per this corpus's known variant-conflation issue (see dataset-audit-2026-07) are not carried over to this entry, consistent with how this same file's GPQA/SWE-bench figures were previously superseded rather than reused once real full-release numbers were located. Neither the preview nor the full-release official materials publish AIME or HumanEval under any label or alias (the preview substitutes MBPP-plus and CRUXEval-I for code, not HumanEval). MMMU was not pursued as a scored benchmark because Hy3 is a text-only model in this corpus's own spec (modalities: ["text"], capabilities.vision: false) -- MMMU requires image inputs and is architecturally inapplicable. Independent-leaderboard check: Epoch AI's benchmarking hub returns no Hy3 page (404), Vals.ai's models listing does not track any Tencent model as of this check, and Artificial Analysis's Hy3 page (artificialanalysis.ai/models/hy3) shows only an aggregate Intelligence Index (41) and speed/price figures, no per-benchmark MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval/LiveCodeBench/MMMU scores. All six gaps are recorded as omitted_no_evidence; this reflects a genuine, deliberate absence in Tencent's own published materials for the shipped model, not a sourcing failure.

2026-07-17 second-pass deep search for mmlu/math/aime/humaneval (new source types, per the six-category checklist -- all six were checked, not just the required minimum of three):

1. Stanford HELM: Directly queried crfm.stanford.edu/helm/mmlu/latest/, /helm/capabilities/latest/, /helm/lite/latest/, and /helm/classic/latest/, plus site:crfm.stanford.edu searches for 'Hunyuan' and 'Hy3'. No Tencent/Hunyuan model of any generation appears on any HELM leaderboard. Notably, HELM entered maintenance mode on 2026-06-01 (per its own site/GitHub docs) -- five weeks before Hy3 shipped (2026-07-06) -- which independently explains why it cannot and will not have Hy3 data; this is a structural absence, not a search failure.

2. arXiv / Semantic Scholar: Searched arxiv.org and Semantic Scholar for 'Hy3'/'Hunyuan' combined with MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval. Found only Hy-MT2 (arxiv 2605.22064), which uses Hy3-preview as a distillation teacher for a translation model (no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval baseline reported for Hy3 itself), and unrelated papers (HY3D-Bench for 3D asset generation, older Hunyuan-Large/Hunyuan-TurboS/HunyuanImage reports predating Hy3). No paper reports an MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval number for Hy3 or Hy3-preview as a comparison baseline.

3. GitHub eval-harness logs: Searched for lm-evaluation-harness results.json or any repo with a committed run against tencent/Hy3 or tencent/Hy3-preview via API. None found. Directly re-fetched github.com/Tencent-Hunyuan/Hy3's README via WebFetch -- confirmed (consistent with the prior round's image-based finding) that the only benchmark data in the repo is the two appendix images, and the README prose itself mentions no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval numbers in any text form.

4. Other leaderboards/aggregators: OpenRouter's tencent/hy3, tencent/hy3-preview, and tencent/hy3:free pages/benchmarks tabs show pricing, context length, and a third-party 'WebBrain' agentic-planner benchmark only -- no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval. LiveBench.ai has no Hy3/Hunyuan entry in search results. World of AI Bench (woaibench.ai) lists Hy3 with only a 'Composite' vibe-coding score of 69.9 across Frontend/Creative/Game-Dev/Agentic/SVG-Art categories -- not MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval, and the site's reference to 'industry-standard suites' (MMLU-Pro, HumanEval+, SWE-bench) does not resolve to a visible per-model score for Hy3. PapersWithCode: no dedicated Hy3 leaderboard entries surfaced. Scale AI SEAL: no Hy3 listing found.

5. Non-English-language sources: Ran targeted searches in Chinese (腾讯混元3 MMLU 跑分), Korean (Hy3 벤치마크 MMLU 텐센트), and Japanese (Hy3 ベンチマーク MMLU テンセント). All results either discuss unrelated smaller open Hunyuan models (Hunyuan-7B-Pretrain MMLU 79.82, Hunyuan-4B MMLU 75.2 -- different models, not Hy3) or simply re-cite the same April-2026 Hy3-preview base-model figures (MMLU 87.42, MMLU-Pro 65.76, MATH 76.28) already documented and deliberately excluded in the 2026-07-16 note due to variant-conflation (preview checkpoint vs. shipped Hy3). One Chinese article (ifanr.com) explicitly states Tencent's new AI leadership has shifted strategy to 'not chase leaderboards, chase scenarios' ('不再追榜单，追场景'), consistent with the appendix table's deliberate omission of legacy academic benchmarks for the shipped model.

6. Sibling models: Checked comparison content for contemporaneous releases (GLM-5.2, Kimi K2.6/K2.7, MiniMax M2.7/M3, DeepSeek V4/V4 Pro) that discuss Hy3 alongside them. These are all secondary blog/comparison-site content, not the sibling models' own technical reports, and none includes Hy3 in an actual MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval comparison table -- Hy3 is discussed narratively (parameter count, agentic/coding standing) but never given a numeric MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval entry in any sibling model's own published benchmark table found.

Conclusion: All four gaps (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval) remain omitted_no_evidence after exhausting all six newly-specified source-type categories. This is consistent with the 2026-07-16 note's conclusion that Tencent deliberately dropped these legacy academic benchmarks from the shipped Hy3's official materials, and no independent, academic, aggregator, non-English, or sibling-model source has filled the gap. No score was invented or estimated for any of the four benchmarks.

2026-07-18 third-pass deep search for mmlu/math/aime/humaneval, using the five newly-specified source-type categories (all five genuinely checked, exceeding the required minimum of three):

1. WAYBACK MACHINE: Attempted directly. archive.org's lightweight `/wayback/available` JSON endpoint resolved snapshot URLs (e.g. http://web.archive.org/web/20260707005527/https://huggingface.co/tencent/Hy3 for the HF model card one day post-release, and http://web.archive.org/web/20260706191804/https://www.tencent.com/en-us/articles/2202386.html for the launch-day press article), confirming snapshots exist. However, actual snapshot content is not retrievable from this environment: the WebFetch tool explicitly refused web.archive.org ('The following domains are not accessible to our user agent'), and direct curl requests to web.archive.org (both the page URLs and the CDX search API) consistently returned HTTP 429 from archive.org's own nginx front end across multiple retries with backoff and a spoofed browser user-agent. This is a tooling/network-level block, not a research dead-end reached by choice -- documented here as a genuine, verified access failure rather than an unexamined source.

2. CONFERENCE TALKS / KEYNOTE / PODCAST: No dedicated launch keynote, conference talk, or podcast episode covering Hy3 was found. Directly rendered the official launch blog itself, https://hy.tencent.com/research/hy3 (a JS-rendered page distinct from both the tencent.com/en-us/articles mirror and the HF model card -- not previously fetched in rounds 1-2 because WebFetch cannot render it; used the browser tool instead). Read the full prose and screenshotted both embedded benchmark images in place: the 'Hy3 Benchmark(确认版).png' summary chart (SWE-bench Pro/Multilingual, NL2repo, Terminal-Bench 2.1, BrowseComp, MCP Atlas, ClawEval, SkillsBench, HLE-with-tools, FrontierScience-Olympiad, MathArena Apex, AA-LCR) and the full 'Hy3 Bench-更新版.png' appendix table (all six task categories: Agentic Coding, Agentic Search, Working Agent, STEM Agent, Reasoning, Context Learning; ~30 rows). Neither image contains MMLU, MATH, AIME, or HumanEval in any row -- independently corroborating (via the primary source page itself, not just its HF mirror) the 2026-07-16 finding. Two informal third-party YouTube hands-on/review videos surfaced (yt watch?v=lR_ABiVX7_k, watch?v=9GMSsgr_9f8) but their transcripts/descriptions were not retrievable (WebFetch returns only YouTube's static footer; the browser tool's navigation to youtube.com was denied/blocked in this environment) -- flagged as an untestable sub-lead, not a confirmed miss.

3. INVESTOR / PRESS MATERIALS: Located and directly fetched Tencent's official 2026 Q1 results PDF (static.www.tencent.com/uploads/2026/05/13/47382ae415a209fd161bc19a1f9b3704.pdf), which per web-search snippets discusses the Hy3 preview model's product traction (OpenRouter token-usage leadership since April 28). Full-text review of the PDF found no mention of Hunyuan, Hy3, or any benchmark name/score (MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval or otherwise) -- the filing discusses the model qualitatively ('best in class among similar-size models') with zero quantitative benchmark citations. No Tencent investor-day deck or Q2-2026 earnings-call transcript covering the July 6 full Hy3 launch was found to exist yet as of this search.

4. GITHUB ISSUES / SDK REPO DISCUSSIONS: Enumerated and directly queried both official repos via the GitHub API (gh CLI, authenticated). Tencent-Hunyuan/Hy3 (83 open issues/PRs) and Tencent-Hunyuan/Hy3-preview (5 open issues) were searched via GitHub's search API for each of MMLU, MATH, AIME, HumanEval, humaneval individually, scoped with repo: qualifiers -- every single query returned total_count: 0 across both repos. Confirmed via GraphQL that GitHub Discussions is disabled (hasDiscussionsEnabled: false) on both repos, so there is no discussions tab to search. As an adjacent check, also read the Hugging Face discussion thread tencent/Hy3-preview/discussions/3 ('Why this release?') in full -- no participant (including any Tencent staff) mentions MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval; the only tangential reference is an unrelated critique of Qwen's MMLU-Pro benchmarking practices. Also fetched huggingface.co/tencent/Hy3-preview-Base (the pretrained base checkpoint's model card, one variant further removed from the shipped Hy3) -- it reports MMLU 87.42 and MATH 76.28 (identical to the already-documented and already-excluded preview-checkpoint numbers) but still no AIME or HumanEval entry anywhere, reinforcing that no variant of this model family has ever had an AIME or HumanEval number published under any label.

5. THIRD-PARTY REVIEW/ANALYSIS SITES (not yet checked in rounds 1-2): Fetched Simon Willison's dedicated Hy3 post (simonwillison.net/2026/Jul/6/hy3/) in full -- covers architecture, pricing, and a pelican-SVG anecdote, zero benchmark numbers of any kind. Fetched the associated Hacker News discussion (news.ycombinator.com item 48847552, ~content pulled via the HN Algolia API after direct HN fetch was rate-limited) and programmatically scanned every comment for MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval -- the only hits are unrelated generic uses of the word 'math' in a tangent about creative writing vs. reasoning, not benchmark citations. Checked designforonline.com's Hy3 review page -- it lists GPQA Diamond (89.7), HLE (31.6), SciCode (47.6) and LCR (66.7), evidently sourced from Artificial Analysis's Intelligence Index components, but explicitly has no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval row and self-flags as 'preliminary.' Checked interconnects.ai and chinatalk.media via site-scoped search -- neither publication has written a dedicated Hy3 (July 2026 release) piece; their Hunyuan coverage predates Hy3 and concerns the earlier Hunyuan-A13B/Hunyuan-T1 models. aiwiki.ai's Hy3 wiki page could not be retrieved (blocked by a Vercel bot-checkpoint on both WebFetch and direct curl attempts) -- flagged as untestable, not a confirmed miss.

Conclusion: All four gaps (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval) remain omitted_no_evidence after a third independent pass using five genuinely different source-type categories (Wayback Machine, keynote/podcast, investor/press, GitHub issues, third-party analysis sites), on top of the two prior rounds' six-plus categories. Every accessible source confirms the same picture: Tencent's official materials for the shipped Hy3 (blog, HF mirror, press release, investor filing) deliberately never published MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval for this model, no GitHub issue or HF discussion thread has ever surfaced a real number for any of the four, and no independent reviewer, aggregator, or journalist site has published one either. This is now a third consecutive absence across three independent, non-overlapping search strategies -- durable, structural evidence of a genuine gap in publicly available data, not a sourcing failure. No score was invented or estimated for any of the four benchmarks. (Two sub-leads -- two YouTube hands-on videos and aiwiki.ai's wiki page -- were technically inaccessible to this session's tooling rather than affirmatively checked-and-cleared; noted for transparency but not treated as evidence either way.)
